prompt:
Task: Make Ion Drift Mobile-Friendly
You are working on Ion Drift, a Rust/React network monitoring application. The frontend is in the web/ directory — React + TypeScript + Vite + TanStack Query, with D3.js for custom visualizations.
Git Setup
bashgit checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b junior/mobile-responsive
Scope
Make the following pages/components work well on mobile viewports (375px–430px width). The goal is demo/showcase quality — someone pulling this up on their phone to show off the tool.
1. Collapsible Sidebar Navigation
The sidebar currently takes up significant screen space. On viewports under 768px:

Collapse the sidebar into a hamburger menu icon (top-left)
Tapping the hamburger opens the sidebar as an overlay (full height, ~280px wide)
Tapping outside the overlay or selecting a nav item closes it
The sidebar should slide in/out with a CSS transition
Keep the existing sidebar behavior on desktop (always visible)

2. Dashboard Page

Cards should stack vertically on mobile (single column)
Stats/metrics should remain readable — no text truncation
Charts (if any Recharts components) should be width-responsive using ResponsiveContainer with 100% width
Maintain visual hierarchy — most important metrics at the top

3. World Map Page

The D3 orthographic projection should resize to fit the viewport width
Touch interactions: pinch-to-zoom and drag-to-rotate should work (D3 zoom behavior supports touch natively, but verify)
Arc labels and tooltips should be readable on small screens
The country list or controls should stack below the map on mobile, not beside it

4. Behavior Page

VLAN accordion sections should be full-width
Anomaly cards should stack vertically
The confirm/flag/dismiss buttons should be touch-friendly (minimum 44px tap targets)
Badge counts should remain visible in the collapsed sidebar/hamburger state
If there are side-by-side layouts, stack them on mobile

Technical Guidelines

Use CSS media queries (@media (max-width: 768px)) or CSS container queries
Prefer CSS-only solutions where possible — avoid JavaScript viewport detection
Do NOT restructure components or change prop interfaces — only add responsive styles and minimal wrapper logic
Do NOT modify any backend Rust code
Do NOT change any API endpoints, data fetching, or TanStack Query hooks
Test mentally at 375px (iPhone SE), 390px (iPhone 14), and 768px (iPad) breakpoints
Preserve all existing desktop behavior — desktop should look identical after your changes

Commit
When complete:
bashgit add -A
git commit -m "feat: mobile-responsive layout for dashboard, sidebar, world map, behavior page"
git push origin junior/mobile-responsive

context_snapshots.README.md:
# ion-drift

Mikrotik RouterOS management dashboard for a single-router homelab. CLI + web UI with Keycloak OIDC authentication.

## Features

- **CLI tool** — query system resources, interfaces, firewall rules, DHCP leases, routes, and logs from the terminal
- **Web dashboard** — React SPA with real-time polling, served by an Axum backend
- **OIDC authentication** — Keycloak integration with PKCE, server-side sessions
- **Private CA support** — trusts certificates from a Smallstep CA
- **Read-only** — monitoring and visibility first; write operations come later

## Quick Start

### CLI

```bash
cargo build --release --bin ion-drift
cp config/cli.example.toml ~/.config/ion-drift/cli.toml
# Edit config with your router details

export HIVE_ROUTER_PASSWORD='your-router-password'
ion-drift system resources
ion-drift interfaces list
ion-drift firewall filter list
```

### Web Server (Docker)

```bash
cp config/server.example.toml config/server.toml
# Edit config with your settings

docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

## Architecture

```
ion-drift/
├── crates/
│   ├── mikrotik-core/      # Shared RouterOS REST API client
│   ├── ion-drift-cli/       # CLI binary (clap)
│   └── ion-drift-web/       # Axum web server
├── web/                     # React frontend (Vite + TypeScript)
├── config/                  # Example configuration files
└── docker/                  # Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml
```

Uses the RouterOS v7 REST API (`/rest/`) with HTTP Basic Auth over HTTPS.

## License

MIT
